message: Morning, team! Anyone else need a triple espresso today?
You're not the only one, Bez! The reports from last week are still giving me nightmares.
Tell me about it, Tinus.
But hey, at least it's sunny outside today. :sunrise:
True, a little sunshine makes everything better.
Also, I'm having my usual green smoothie. That's my secret energy booster.
Nice, Bez! I'll stick with my coffee though.
Anyone else excited for the launch of the new BitCoin :coin: payment feature this week?
Absolutely, Daniel! Been looking forward to it.
By the way, no spoilers, but did anyone catch the football game? Epic comeback!
I missed it! I was knee deep in a sci-fi novel. Anyone else into that?
Oh, I love sci-fi! Can you share the name of the book, Shay?
I've been reading a bit of sci-fi myself.
Looking forward to sharing recommendations, Shay!
There's always so much happening outside of work! Keep the energy :battery: going, team.
It's going to be another productive day. :raised_hands:

Welcome to The Chain, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

? *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands*
 ? *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin Start button
Clicked Start button
:alarm_clock: - You have 25 minutes to resolve the outage, good luck! You can type `/help` if you need any reminders.
Hey, we're getting reports of customers struggling to make purchases
@here Anyone else seeing this?
Yes, I've noticed the same, @Bob . It seems like an error in the system
Whatever it is, we need to sort it out quickly.
we need to step on it! before the social media backlash is turning into a storm.
Who is handling incidents today? Please size up the issue, this is urgent
Bob Can you confirm if this is affecting customers in all regions?
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
sure let me take a look into the reports for you now
Tinus I am handling incidents today
Daniel Can you investigate these errors being reported?
From the looks of it, this issue is only being reported on withing the whole of the EU
Yes i can take a look into the logs for you now
Sev-2 Customers in EU unable to make payments
:star: Incident record created
*Incident Number INC100099:* Sev-2 Customers in EU unable to make payments
:star: Send first comms in first 5 minutes
:star: Qualify the problem
It seems there are errors in a few services, payments and in cart service
Tanya Can you investigate if there are any infrastructure issues that could be contributing to this issue?
:star: Form an effective investigation team
i could restart the cart service and see if this fixes things?
it seems like payment service is causing a lot of issues from what i see
We are just confirming if this is application or infrastructure related and will report back with any further updates as information becomes available.
ok lets get all hands on this
keep me updated with any new developments, investors will be asking
customers aren't happy, they are asking for an eta on when this will be fixed
Daniel and Tanya can you work together to resolve this issue?
i think we should take a look into the changes to see if any recent deployments are impacting this
good idea, i will go and grab the document with all the recent deployments

Here we go:
<https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ULPUBLIC/pages/1114341391>
Bob please advise customer that we are aware of the issue and are investigating how we can resolve it.
there is a lot of changes made today, Graham can you take a look at these aswell, we need your insite on what you think this could be
i will check them aswell
daniel i see you made a change to payment service, what's this about ?
Tanya and Daniel Could this be related to the redis deployment that went out before the PaymentService?
:star: Reviewed changes
let me check the redis change
its Bitcoin payment handling and transaction logging updates
how are we holding up?
:bulb: keep the business informed regularly
Bob Can you confirm what payment method customer are using when the failures occur?
this redis change doesnt seem to be throwing up any errors, we should look into the payment change, its logs seems to be where this is originating from
within the EU it seems to be impacting all users, i doubt all them are using bitcoin
We believe this could be related to some recent deployments and are just trying to rule out which deployments could have caused the issue.
its possible daniels change had broken all of payment service within the EU, daniel care to weigh in
Yes now that i think of it the bitcoin change is only deployed for the EU, this has to be the issue
that would explain the errors in other services as they are connected
Daniel Can your rollback your changes to see if that resolves the issue?
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
and why all of payment service is down
Understood, I'll proceed with rolling back the payment service
payment service rollback is complete
Bob please advise customers we have identified the cause of the issue and are working to restore service as soon as possible
:star: Know your team and their roles
This is not good. Something is broken after the payment rollback
not looking good, this issue seems to be global now
putting out a message to respond now
social media is blowing up, has this gotten worse?
Hamed can you provide further details so that Daniel and Tanya can investigate further?
not sure what has made this worse, there are no error in payment service now
let me and tanya look into this
on it
ok it seem that the cart redis and service are dependant on the payments change
This incident is now a sev-1 as this affecting all customers globally.
:star: Formally assign criticality level to the incident
daniel overlooked this detail when rolling back the change
my bad, what can we do now?
Hamed could this be resolved by rolling back changes to those services too?
:star: Form a working theory
Yes, we will need to rollback the remaining 2 deployments and potentially restart the services yes
Daniel and Tanya please start rolling back those deployments and restart the services
on it
i will help daniel
:star: Take command
Rollbacks are completed
Great, i've also restarted the frontend so the website should be working now
@graham.desouza can you confirm?
We have started rolling back 2 other services and expect to restore service shortly.
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
:alarm_clock: - You have less than 4 minutes. You can type `/help` if you need any reminders.
Tinus The website still displays an error message when trying to checkout and item
frontend has been restarted, took a second there
let me take a look at the site now
lets hope this works
Tinus the checout is working again
The website appears to be working again
It appears that everything has returned to its normal state
I was worried for a bit but everything looks good now.
Great work, everyone! The payments issue has been resolved and customers can now check out smoothly
Phew! Glad we got that sorted. Fantastic teamwork!
Absolutely! Proud to be a part of this team
Thank you @graham.desouza for coordinating the rollback effectively
Brilliant job, team and @graham.desouza!
I've been monitoring the financial metrics, and they're already improving
